trace_table.claim: Back the women who will run the region's aviation.
trace_table.vault_source: 02 Platform/02 Public Website Content - English (Draft).md, Get involved / Partners (corporate), Page heading

trace_table.claim: A company that partners with WAI-ME gets credible, third-party proof that it backs women in aviation, issued through a chapter of an internationally recognised body, not its own marketing. It gets early access to a growing pool of regional talent. And it gets a yearly impact story it can show its own board, its people, and the public.
trace_table.vault_source: 02 Platform/02 Public Website Content - English (Draft).md, Get involved / Partners (corporate), Why partner with WAI-ME

trace_table.claim: WAI-ME is the Middle East chapter of Women in Aviation International, operating since 2013 in association with The Emirates Aerosports Federation. A company is not backing an unknown local group. It is partnering with the regional arm of a global name, in step with the UAE's own priorities on advancing women and national talent.
trace_table.vault_source: 02 Platform/02 Public Website Content - English (Draft).md, Get involved / Partners (corporate), Why partner with WAI-ME; 01 Organization/01.02 Membership Model/01.02 Corporate Membership - Decision & Handoff.md

trace_table.claim: WAI-ME handles no cash. A partner commits a set amount of support each year and delivers it directly, by funding a scholarship or training, running a workshop, offering internships, or paying a WAI-ME cost straight to the supplier. Every dirham goes to the beneficiary, the student, the trainer, the venue, with nothing held in between. It is a cleaner story than pooling donations, and it is the model we are proud of.
trace_table.vault_source: 02 Platform/02 Public Website Content - English (Draft).md, Get involved / Partners (corporate), How the support works; 01 Organization/01.02 Membership Model/01.02 Corporate Money Mechanism (Decision).md

trace_table.claim: The public page shows Supporter, Partner, and Champion as the three corporate tiers, without AED figures.
trace_table.vault_source: 02 Platform/02 Public Website Content - English (Draft).md, Get involved / Partners (corporate), The three tiers, DECIDED 2026-06-27; 01 Organization/01.02 Membership Model/01.02 Corporate Membership - Decision & Handoff.md

trace_table.claim: Supporter: A company beginning its commitment to women in aviation: the partner seal for the year, named recognition, and a share in the yearly impact report.
trace_table.vault_source: 02 Platform/02 Public Website Content - English (Draft).md, Get involved / Partners (corporate), The three tiers

trace_table.claim: Partner: A company backing real outcomes: everything in Supporter, plus a larger role at events and in the talent pipeline.
trace_table.vault_source: 02 Platform/02 Public Website Content - English (Draft).md, Get involved / Partners (corporate), The three tiers

trace_table.claim: Champion: A company funding life-changing access: everything in Partner, plus headline recognition and enough to fund a full pilot licence or several scholarships at our partner academies.
trace_table.vault_source: 02 Platform/02 Public Website Content - English (Draft).md, Get involved / Partners (corporate), The three tiers, DECIDED 2026-06-28

trace_table.claim: Every tier carries an internship commitment, so partners help build the pipeline, not just fund it.

trace_table.claim: Partnerships are agreed by a simple Memorandum of Understanding, signed by our President. There is no binding contract and no money paid to WAI-ME. To start a conversation, email us and we will walk you through it.

summary: Built dist/get-involved/index.html traces to the governing vault notes, including the shortened Champion copy and the no-AED public-tier decision. Brand locks hold, and no staging allowances are needed for this page.
